runs.interpretation: Primary-earner substitution-response elasticity for earnings decile 9 in a U.S. microsimulation model.
citations: Standard labor-supply elasticity ranges used in tax-benefit microsimulations
Evidence that substitution/participation responses vary by income (higher for upper-middle/moderate deciles than for bottom deciles)

runs.reasoningSummary: I place this decile’s primary-earner substitution-response elasticity moderately high but not extreme, reflecting a typical positive labor-supply/substitution response that varies with income and remains in the ~0.5–1.5 range for many tax-benefit simulations; the median is set at 0.9.
